context:
This is **core.lib**: the shared Angular Material/theme component library for the `*.web` apps (workspace.web, wms.web, core.web, ads.web, and a social app now consuming it). It is a library, not an app ‚Äî everything here ships to other teams.

- Angular 21 workspace, CLI 21.2. The library is the `ui` project at `projects/ui`. There is no application project.
- Angular Material + CDK **v21** (pinned to match the workspace ‚Äî npm's latest is v22 and needs Angular 22; do not bump it here).
- The shared M3 theme at `projects/ui/src/styles/_theme.scss` is the single source of truth for palette, typography and density across the whole fleet. Build on Material and this theme: never hand-roll what the theme should own, and never hardcode a colour.
  - Material's `color` input is an **M2-only API and does nothing under an M3 theme**. Resolve colour via `mat.*-overrides()` against `--mat-sys-*` system tokens instead ‚Äî see `projects/ui/src/styles/_button.scss` for the established pattern.
  - A directive cannot carry a `styleUrl`, so a directive's theming lives in a partial under `src/styles/` that `_theme.scss` `@use`s ‚Äî again, see `_button.scss`.
  - The theme also emits `--ui-sys-success/warning` roles that M3 itself lacks. Use them rather than inventing a parallel colour system.
- Export every public surface from `projects/ui/src/public-api.ts`, or consumers cannot import it and it is dead code.
- **Match the closest existing implementation ‚Äî do not invent a new shape.** Pick the exemplar by what you are building:
  - Decorating a native element ‚Üí `lib/button/`. A directive on the native element, theming via system tokens in `styles/_button.scss`. This is the canonical reference for conventions and for story/spec style.
  - A form control ‚Üí `lib/input/` or `lib/select/`. Every form control implements `ControlValueAccessor` (and `validate()` where validation applies) so `[(ngModel)]` and reactive forms work with no adapter. `lib/checkbox/`, `lib/radio-group/`, `lib/toggle/`, `lib/textarea/`, `lib/autocomplete/` and `lib/datepicker/` all already follow it ‚Äî copy one rather than re-deriving it.
  - An imperative or overlay API ‚Üí `lib/snackbar/`, which is an injectable service, not a component.
  - Something that genuinely owns structure/composition ‚Üí `lib/card/`.
- The library is **published as `@w-industries/ui` to GitHub Packages**, so the public API is a real contract: a breaking change to an existing surface costs consumers a migration. Prefer additive changes, and treat a rename of an exported symbol as a breaking change.
- Every component ships a `.stories.ts` (CSF3) with one story per meaningful configuration. The published Storybook (https://w-industries-luke.github.io/core.lib/) is how app teams discover what exists, so an undocumented variant may as well not exist.
- Tests run on vitest via `@angular/build:unit-test` ‚Äî no browser needed. `npm run test:a11y` additionally renders every story through axe.
- Do not edit `.github/workflows` unless the task explicitly says you may.
